ety: v.n. fr. يمن 'to be fortunate and happy'
def: s.m. Felicity, prosperity; good luck; — a token for good.
more: (Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Felicity, prosperity, good luck. ¶ Yaman, n. prop. Name of a country, Arabia Felix.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یمن (yaman), from Arabic اَلْيَمَن (al-yaman).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = yāṇ, q.v.
more: Thus, in this manner. يون تون yoṅ toṅ, In various ways, somehow or other. يون نه يون yuṅ na yuṅ, One way or other, somehow or other. يونہين yūṅhīṅ or yoṅhīṅ, In this very manner, thus, accidentally, by chance, causelessly, easily, cursorily.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit यवन (yavana), itself from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑁄𑀦 (yona), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎢𐎴 (y-u-n /⁠yauna⁠/). Doublet of یونان (yūnān) and جون (javan).

def: adv. S. रावम् Thus; in this manner. Yāṅ yūṅ bhī vāh vāh hai aur vūṅ bhī vāh vāh! Just so, bravo! even so bravo! yūṅ to , adv. 1. In this case. 2. Speaking generally. yūṅ tūṅ k. , v. a. Polite for to abuse, curse. yūṅ na yūṅ , adv. 1. One way or the other; somehow or other. 2. In no wise. yūṅ'hī, yūṅhīṅ, yoṅhī , adv. 1. In this way; thus. Tum to yūṅhī kahā karte ho . 2. Easily; cursorily. 3. By chance. 4. Causelessly ( be-sabab ). yūṅ hī sahī . Be it so! all right! Jo kahoge tum kaheṅge ham bhī, hāṅ, yūṅ hī sahī, Yāṅ khushī hai āp kī to, mehr-bāṅ, yūṅ hī sahī . Whate'er you say I will say too, yes, even so, Whate'er your pleasure is, my friend, e'en thus I'll do.

ety: for एउँ = Ap. Prk. एव or एम्व = S. इयत् (Vedic ईवत्)
def: adv. Thus, in this wise, in this manner; — just so, for no particular reason; without just ground, vainly, idly, causelessly, gratuitously; to please oneself
subs: adv. In this case; — generally speaking
adv. One way or other; somehow or other; — in no wise
yoṅ-hī, or yūṅ-hīṅ, yoṅ-hīṅ, adv. In this very manner, exactly thus; — without any apparent cause or reason, causelessly, &c. (i.q. yūṅ, q.v.); by chance, accidently; — cursorily, easily
adv. Be it so then; just as you please.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit यवन (yavana), itself from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑁄𑀦 (yona), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎢𐎴 (y-u-n /⁠yauna⁠/). Doublet of یونان (yūnān) and جون (javan).

def: Jonah, Jonas (prophet); Book of Jonah
wety: Borrowed from Hebrew יונה (yônā).

def: United Kingdom (a kingdom and country in Northern Europe)
wety: Borrowed from English United Kingdom.

def: prop. n. Ionia; Greece (so called till subdued by the Romans; since then the term Rūm has been applied to the Eastern Roman empire at large).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یونان (yunān), ultimately from Ancient Greek Ἰωνία (Iōnía, “Ionia”).

ety: rel. n. fr. yūnān
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Yūnān; Ionian, Grecian, Greek; — an Ionian, a Grecian; — s.f. The Greek language
more: adj. Grecian; Greek. yūnānī tib , The Greek system of medicine.
subs: s.f. The Greek system of medicine.

def: adj. = yūnānī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Yūnān; Ionian, Grecian, Greek; — an Ionian, a Grecian; — s.f. The Greek language
subs: s.f. The Greek system of medicine.

def: s.m. pl. (of Yūnānī), The Greeks.
s.m. Jonah (the prophet).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic يُونُس (yūnus), from Ancient Greek Ἰωνᾶς (Iōnâs), from Hebrew יונה (yônā).

ety: S. यूनी
def: s.f. = yuvatī, q.v. s.f. A young woman (from 16 to 30 years of age), a damsel (see juvatī).
union territory (an administrative division of India that is ruled directly by the federal government, as opposed to a state or province)
wety: یُونِیَن (yūniyan, “union”) + عِلاقَہ ('ilāqa, “area, territory”).

def: s.m. See the pop. form جوپ jūp.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. Europe
wety: Borrowed from English Europe, ultimately from Ancient Greek Εὐρώπη (Eurṓpē).

def: European Union
s.f. Invasion; assault, attack, storm
more: s. m. Assault, storm, invasion. يورش کرنا yūrish karnā, To invade.
subs: To make an attack (on), to invade, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یورش (yūriš), from Chagatai یورش (yöriš).

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. Europe ( = yūrap).
adj. European.
s.m. See the pop. form جوس jūs.
s.m. The patriarch Joseph; — a proper name.
more: To add, Gen. xxx. 24.) n. prop. The patriarch Joseph.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian یُوسُف (yūsuf), borrowed from Arabic يُوسُف (yūsuf), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew יוֹסֵף (yōsép), from הוֹסִיף (hosíf, “to add”).

def: exceedingly beautiful, handsome
ex:
لب شیریں ہے مصری یوسف ثانی ہے یہ لڑکا
نہ چھوڑے گا میرا دل چاہ کنعانی ہے یہ لڑکا
lab-e-śīrī̃ hai misrī yūsuf-e-sānī hai ye laṛkā
na choṛegā merā dil cāh-e-kan'ānī hai ye laṛkā
(please add an English translation of this quotation)
wety: Ezafe compound of یوسف (yūsuf, “Joseph”) + ثانی (sānī, “second”), literally “the second Joseph”.

ety: rt. yu
def: part. (used as a suff.), Attached (to); united, or connected (with), joined (to); in contact (with); accompanied, attended (by); with; possessed (of), furnished (with), endowed (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـِیَّت (-iyyat), in turn from Arabic ـِيَّة (-iyya). Compare Hindi -ता (-tā) and Hindi -त्व (-tva).

def: s.m. corr. of يوتهہ yūth, q.v.

ety: S. यूथ+कः
def: s.m. = yūth, q.v.
s.m. A flock, herd, drove (of birds, animals, &c.), a multitude, number; a body, troop, band, company (see jūth)
subs: vulg. yūthap, or yūth-pati, or yūth-nāth, s.m. Keeper or protector of a troop; lord or captain of a band; leader or head of a flock or herd; — a large elephant (the leader of a wild herd)

def: s.m. See s.v. yūtha or yūth. s.m. A flock, herd, drove (of birds, animals, &c.), a multitude, number; a body, troop, band, company (see jūth)
subs: vulg. yūthap, or yūth-pati, or yūth-nāth, s.m. Keeper or protector of a troop; lord or captain of a band; leader or head of a flock or herd; — a large elephant (the leader of a wild herd)

ety: S. यूथिका
def: s.f. A kind of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum (see jūhī, or jūʼī, the pop. forms).
s.m. (dialec.) = jūʼā, 'yoke,' q.v. s.m. A yoke; the yoke of a carriage or plough
subs: To throw off the yoke, to refuse the yoke; to be tired (of).

ety: base yuvan
def: adj. & s.m. (f. yuvati, or yuvatī), adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; arrived at puberty, adult, adolescent; — strong, vigorous, healthy; — one who has attained the age of puberty, a young man, a youth (from 16 to 17 years of age; — see the pop. form juvā)
subs: s.m., and yuva-rājya, s.m. See s.v. juvā.

ety: base of yuvā
def: adj. = yuvā, q.v. (cf. jawān). s.m. (dialec.) = jūʼā, 'yoke,' q.v. s.m. A yoke; the yoke of a carriage or plough
subs: To throw off the yoke, to refuse the yoke; to be tired (of).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit यवन (yavana), itself from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀬𑁄𑀦 (yona), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎢𐎴 (y-u-n /⁠yauna⁠/). Doublet of یونان (yūnān) and جون (javan).

ety: yuvā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The state or condition of puberty, youth (regarded as a state or condition, or as a time of life), youthfulness, adolescence.
s.m. See juva-rāj, s.v. juva.
s.f. Youthfulness, youth, adolescence.

ety: = yuvā, q.v.
def: s.f. A young woman (from 16 to 30 years of age), a damsel (see juvatī).
more: s. f. See s. جوتي juvatī.

ety: S. युवति+इनी
def: s.f. = يوتي yuvatī, q.v. s.f. A young woman (from 16 to 30 years of age), a damsel (see juvatī).
s.m. = yuvatā, q.v. s.f. Youthfulness, youth, adolescence.
s.m. A hunting-leopard (syn. ćītā); — a small species of panther.
contrac. of zād or zāda, q.v. (used in comp., e.g. mīr-zā or mirzā, q.v.).
more: p. p. ( In comp .) Born; as ādam-zād born of man, shah-zāda a prince. zād-būm; H. janam bhūm , n. f. Birthplace; native land. zād-rāh , n. m. Travelling expenses; way charges.
in compos. for زاده zāda, Born; son.

def: alternative form of زَبان (zabān, “tongue; language”)
s.m.? Civet (the perfume so called).
more: s. Civet, the perfume so called.

ety: Parsī hizvãn; Pehl. huzvān; Zend hizva; S. जिव्हा
def: s.f. The tongue (syn. jībh); tongue, speech, language, dialect (syn. bolī)
more: n. f. Z. husvān, hozvān ; Pahlvī, zafān ; S. jihvā ; H. jībh . 1. The tongue. 1. Sāre badan meṅ zabān halāl hai . Prov. The tongue is the purest member of the body. 2. Itnī sī jān, gaz bhar kī zabān! Prov. So young, a yard of tongue! 3. Mārte ke hāth pakṛe jāṭe haiṅ, kahte kī zabān nahīṅ pakṛī jātī . Prov. You may hold the hand that strikes, you cannot hold the tongue. 4. Jiskī zabān chale, uske sattar kai chaleṅ . Prov. The power of the tongue is as the power of seventy ploughs. 2. Language ( bolī 3.). zabān baṅd k. , v. a. To hush; silence; shut up; floor. zabān band h. , v. n. 1. To have lockjaw. 2. To be unable to speak. zabān-bandī , n. f. A deposition; a written testimony; an affidavit (in a court of law). zabān-bandī k. , v. a. To take down a deposition. zabān par chaṛhnā , v. n. 1. To be on one's tongue or on the lips. Yeh kyā burā lafz tumhārī zabān par chaṛh gayā hai . 2. To be…

s. f. 1. The tongue. 2. Language, dialect, speech. 3. Flame (of a candle, &c.). زبان آور zabān-āwar, Eloquent, fluent, voluble. زبان آوري zabān-āwar-ī, s. f. Eloquence, fluency, volubility of tongue. زبان بڙهانا zabān baṛhānā, To chatter, to prattle. زبان پرچڙهنا zabān par chaṛhnā, 1. To be publick. 2. To learn by heart. زبان پرلانا zabān par lānā, To speak, say, mention. زبان پکڙنا zabān pakaṛnā, To prevent one from speaking, to criticise. زبان پلٿنا zabān palaṭnā, To equivocate, to retract, to eat oneʼs words. زبان چلاني zabān chalānī, To give abuse. زبان دابنا zabān dābnā, To hold oneʼs tongue. زبان دابکي کـہنا zabān dābke kahnā, To detract in whispers. زبان دان zabān-dān, Skilled in language; a poet, a linguist. زبان داني zabān-dān-ī, s. f. Knowledge of language. زبان دراز zabān-darāz, adj. Abusive. زبان درازي zabān-darāz-ī, s. f. Abuse. زبان درازي کرنا zabān-darāzī karnā, To give ab…
subs: s.f. Eloquence, fluency, volubility of tongue
To let the tongue wag, to chatter, prattle
v.n. To be speechless, be unable to speak; to be stricken dumb; — to have lockjaw
s.f. Written testimony, deposition, affidavit (in a court of law)
v.t. To take down a deposition
(-kī), To obtain the assent, or concurrence, or approval (of)
(-kī), To be on the tongue (of), be talked about, be public; to be on the tip of (one's) tongue, to have or know (a thing) by heart
v.t 'To put on the tongue,' to taste
(-ko), To utter, say, speak of, mention
(-kī), lit. 'To lay hold of the tongue' (of); to prevent (one) from speaking; to interrupt; to seize on the utterances (of), to criticise, to cavil (at)
or zabān phernā, To retract, to eat one's words; to equivocate, to prevaricate, to shuffle
(-kī), The tongue to be exercised in correct articulation; to be able to articulate or pronounce correctly, to speak with correct articulation
(apnī), To talk much, talk at a great rate; to use foul language, to give abuse
To live by a glib use of the tongue, or by flattery
To speak with bated breath; to detract in whispers
adj. & s.m. Skilled in language; — one well-versed in a language; a poet; a scholar; a linguist
s.f. Knowledge of a language, linguistic attainments
adj. 'Long-tongued'; loquacious; speaking much and malevolently; impudent, saucy; abusive, scurrilous
(-ko), To show the tongue (to a physician)
(-meṅ), To interfere by speech, to inquire, ask, request
adj. Talked about, spoken of, reported, public; current, used, in use
(-ke), To be talked about, be currently reported, be often mentioned; to become current (as speech), to be generally used (by), be commonly employed (by), to pass into an idiom
To be careful as to what one says, to speak carefully or cautiously
(apnī), To control (one's) tongue, to be careful as to how one speaks or what one says; to hold one's tongue
v.t. To give utterance to, to utter, pronounce, speak
(-kī), To escape the lips (of); to be uttered, be said

(-kī), To interrupt the speech (of); to make a clicking noise with the (apnī) tongue expressive of regret at a slip or error; to be sorry for what one has said; to grieve, lament; to speak foolishly or inconsiderately
or zabān-ko muṅh-meṅ rakhnā, To keep the tongue under control; to hold one's tongue, to be silent
(apnī), To give loose to the tongue; to speak out
To smack the lips (over), to enjoy or relish; to enjoy dainties
(apnī), To wag the tongue, to speak; to ask, request.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زبان (zabān, zubān), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *hijwáH, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ȷ́iȷ́ʰwáH. First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi زبان (zban /⁠zabān⁠/),. Doublet of جِیبھ (jībh).

def: s.m. Tongue (of fire, or of a buckle, &c.), flame (of a candle).
more: s. m. Flame (particularly, of a candle).

def: direct plural of زُبان (zubān)

ety: of, -kī
def: adv. By word, orally, &c.
more: adj. 1. By word of mouth; verbal; oral; viva voce . 2. Unwritten; traditional. zabānī imtehān , Oral examination. zabānī paṛhnā , v. a. To read or recite. zabānī jamakharch , Mere words; words, not deeds.
1. s. f. From the tongue, by the mouth, language. 2. adj. Traditional, oral, verbal, by word, of or by or from the tongue.

def: oblique plural of زُبان (zubān)
vocative plural of زُبان (zubān)

ety: az+bar, 'upon,' qq.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Above, upper, high, superior; greater, larger; — the Persian term for the short vowel َ a (so called because it is placed over a letter; — the A. syn. is fatḥa)
more: adj. زبر became large. 1. See bhārī (1-4, 8, 9, 15, 19.). 2. ( zabar-jang ) Big; great; strong. Ghar taṅg, bahū zabar jaṅg . Prov. The but small, the daughter-in-law tall. 3. Higher; above; superior. 4. Greater; larger. zabar, fatah , n. m. The vowel point (') representing a short alif (a) in bal strength. zabar h. , v. n. To have the upper hand.

1. adj. Above, superiour. 2. s. m. The vowel point (َ) pronounced a. زبردست zabar-dast, adj. Vigorous, violent, powerful, victorious, oppressive.زبردستي zabar-dast-ī, 1. s. f. Violence, powerfulness, oppression. 2. adv. Violently, forcibly. زبردستي کرنا zabar dast-ī karnā, To oppress, to force. زبرکرنا zabar karnā, To oppress. زبرهونا zabar honā, To have the upper hand. ¶ زبر zibbir, adj. Powerful, potent.
subs: adj. Having the upper hand, superior, victorious; vigorous, powerful, strong, athletic; high-handed, overbearing, tyrannical, harsh, oppressive, violent
s.f. Superiority; powerfulness; high-handedness, oppression, tyranny, violence, force; — adv. By violence or force, forcibly, violently, wrongfully, unjustly, arbitrarily, wantonly
(-se, or -par), To use violence or force (with or towards), to force, compel; to oppress
v.n. To be superior, to have the upper hand.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَبَر (zabar).

def: adj. 1. Strong ( balvān . 1.); having the upper hand. 2. Stronger. 3. More able; superior. 4. High-handed; violent; oppressive. Zabar-dast māre aur rone na de . Prov. The tyrant smites his subjects down, nor lets his victims moan or groan.
adj. Upper-handed, superiour.

n. f. See bārā-jorī. zabar-dastī, zabar-dastī se , adv. 1. By force or violence; forcibly. 2. Wrongfully; unjustly; per fas et nefas ; arbitrarily. 3. Wantonly. zabar-dastī bhagā le jānā . Forcible abduction. zabar dastī pakaṛ bulānā , v. a. To bring one by force. zabar-dastī chaṛh baiṭhnā , v. a. To commit an indecent assault; to rape. zabar-dastī chhīn lenā, yā le lenā , v. a. To take by force, or snatch from; to extort. zabar-dastī hāzir k . To enforce the production of; to compel one's attendance. zabar-dastī rok rakhnā , v. n. To keep back by force; withhold wrongfully. zabar-dastī izzat lenā , v. a. To make an indecent assault. zabar-dastī qabūl karvānā, yā qubulvānā , v. a. To extort confession. zabar-dastī k. , v. a. 1. To use force; to force; compel; oblige. 2. To press or bear hard upon; to oppress.
wety: From زبردست (zabardast, “superior, powerful”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Violence, oppression.

ety: zabar, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Higher, upper; superior.
s.m. A kind of emerald, a chrysolite; a topaz; a beryl; jasper.
more: s. m. A kind of emerald, a chrysolite, a topaz, a beryl, jasper.

ety: act. part. n. of ذبح 'to cut, cleave, slaughter'
def: s.m. Slaughterer, sacrificer; butcher
more: n. m. ذبح cleaving, slaughtering. A butcher.
part. act. (of ذبح) A sacrificer, a butcher. سعد الذابح saﻌdu-ẕ-ẕābiḥ, Two bright stars in the left horn of Capricorn.
subs: The two stars α and β in the left horn of Capricornus; the twenty-second mansion of the moon.

ety: inf. n. of ذبح 'to slaughter,' &c.
def: s.f. Slaughtering, cutting the throat of, slaying, sacrificing; — ẕibḥ, vulg. ẕibaḥ, s.f. An animal prepared for slaughter, or sacrifice; a sacrifice, a slaughter
more: n. m. A sacrifice; slaughter. zibah k. , Mah. v. a. 1. To slaughter; kill; sacrifice. Mere sāmne bolā to zibah kar ḍalūṅgā . 2. To immolate.

s. f. (Heb. ) A sacrifice, slaughter. ذبح کرنا ẕabḥ karnā, 1. To sacrifice, to immolate. 2. To kill (animals for food agreeably to the Muhammadan law), to slaughter. ذبح هونا ẕabḥ honā, To be sacrificed or killed, &c.
subs: To slaughter, cut the throat of (an animal intended for food, agreeably to the Moḥammadan law); to sacrifice, to immolate; to slay, kill, execute, put to death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذِبْح (zibh), from Arabic ذَبْح (ḏabḥ).

ety: for A. z̤abyuṉ
def: s.m. The gazelle, the Arabian antelope; a deer, a chevril.

ety: v.n. fr. ذبح; and = maẕbūḥ
def: part. adj. Slaughtered, sacrificed; — s.m. An animal that is destined or prepared for sacrifice, an intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥa)
more: (from ذبح) 1. part. Sacrificed. 2. s. m. A sacrifice. ذبيح الله ẕabīḥu-l-lāh, A name of Ismāʼīﻌl, the son of Ibrāhīm.
subs: A surname of Ishmael (who, according to the generality of Moḥammadans, was the son whom Abraham designed to sacrifice, and not Isaac).

ety: for A. ذبيحة ẕabīḥat, fem. of ẕabīḥ
def: s.m. An intended victim, a sacrifice ( = ẕabīḥ); an animal lawfully slaughtered (and fit for food).
more: n. m. An animal lawfully slaughtered and fit for food (Mah).

def: (act. part. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve,' &c.), Careful, cautious, provident, prudent; practising self-control, self-denying, abstemious, continent; — exact, correct, accurate, strict; punctual; — restrainer; possessor, master, ruler, governor
more: adj. 1. Strict; punctual. 2. Self-denying; abstemious; continent. 3. Patient; enduring.
(part. act. of ضبط) s. m. A governour, master, possessor, restrainer. ضابط الکلّ ẓābit̤u-l-kull, Omnipotent (God), lord of all.
subs: Lord or ruler of all; the Omnipotent.

ety: for A. ضابطة, fem. of ẓābit̤, q.v.
def: s.m. Universal rule, general canon, established practice; rule, canon, law, ordinance, regulation; custom; judicial usage; manner of proceeding, procedure
more: s. m. Canon, ordinance, law, rule, regulation. (Plur. ضوابط).
subs: (-kā), To employ legal means, take legal measures; to act according to law
or ḥasb-ě-ẓābit̤a, adv. According to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course
s.m. A body of instructions for revenue officers
adj. and adv. Regular, formal, en règle; — according to rule or established practice; through the regular channel; in due course, duly
adj. Irregular, contrary to rule, informal, unusual.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَابِطَه (zābita), from Arabic ضَابِطَة (ḍābiṭa).

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Being according to law or rule; conformity to law or rule, regularity
more: n. f. Conformity to rule or law. be-zābtagī , n. f. Informality; irregularity. be-zābtagī kī sūrat . Want of form. be-zābtagī-i-nālish . Informality of procedure.

ety: inf. n. of ضبط 'to keep, preserve'; 'to withhold, restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Keeping, taking care of, guarding, defending, watching over, ruling, governing; regulation, government, direction, discipline; restraint, control, check; taking possession of, possession, seizure, confiscation, sequestration, resumption
more: n. m. zabtī , n. f. ضبط held fast. 1. ( zabt ) Control; restraint. 2. The act of confiscating. 3. Seizure; confiscation; resumption; sequestration. zabt k. , v. a. 1. To control; restrain; check (passion). Āh karne men shān jātī hai, Zabt karne meṅ jān jātī hai . One's dignity with sighing goes, One's life in patient suffering goes. 2. To seize or attach property; confiscate; resume (an estate). zabt h., zabtī meṅ ānā , v. n. To be seized, confiscated. zabtī arāzī-i-lā-khirāj yā muāfī . r: ẓād, ẓwād, ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a, z, d
def: (called ẓād-ě-mǒʻjama, or ẓād-ě-manqūt̤a), is the twenty-first letter of the Urdū alphabet (the fifteenth of the Arabic, from which it is taken); — it occurs only in words borrowed from the Arabic and the secondary formations therefrom. In Hindūstānī (and Persian) it is pronounced like ز z; but in Arabic it is a strongly articulated palatal d. As a numeral it denotes 800.
more: called ضادمعجمه and ضادمنقوطه ز the 15th letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in Persian, and has no corresponding letter in Sanskrit or Hindi. In Persia and Hindustān its pronounciation is hardly distinguished from that of ز or ذ but in Arabia it is a mixed sound of d , the, and w , only to be acquired from natives of that country. In abjad , q. v. it stands for 800.

the fifteenth letter of the Arabic alphabet, does not occur in Persian, nor is there any corresponding letter in Sanskrit. In Persia and Hindūstān its pronunciation is hardly distinguished from that of ز; but, in Arabia it is a mixed sound of d, th, and w, only to be acquired from natives of that country. In the notation of numbers by the Arabic letters, it stands for 800. It is one of the radical letters. The د of Persian words, when they are introduced into Arabic, is often changed into this letter, as ضحاک for ده آک See under ح. When three of these letters meet together, as in تقضّض the darting of a hawk upon his prey, the last is changed into ي, as تقضي. And in verbs of the measure افتعال the servile ت after this letter is changed to ط or to ض as اضطراب or اضّراب from ضرب.

def: n. m. 1. Poison (بس). 2. ( zahr-bād ) The bitter principle. zahr , adj. 1. Bitter; bitter as gall. 2. Pernicious. 3. Hateful; abominable; odious; obnoxious. Zahr kar detī hai woh khāne ko laṛ-ke mujh se roz, Āj se maiṅ sāth uske khānā khāūṅ dūr pār! Wom. Rangīn. My meals to poison she just turns, she fights with me each day, Out on it if a morsel I do touch e'en from today! zahr ugalnā , v. n. See bis ulagnā. zahr-ālūdah , adj. Impregnated with poison; poisonous. zahr-bād , n. m. A dangerous disease in horses and elephants in which the yard and testicles of the animal become suddenly swollen and violently inflamed. zahr-bād chaṛhnā , v. n. To circulate in the body (poison). zahr-dār , adj. Poisonous. zahr-i-qātil, zahr-i-halāhal , A deadly poison. zahr khānā , v. n. To take poison ( bis khānā ). zahr khānā kisī par . To take poison on account of one (through love, jealousy, shame, etc.); to perform the happy despatch. zahr kī se ghūṅṭ pīnā , v. n. lit . to gulp down as poison. To suppress one's feelings; to bear patiently. zahr lagnā , v. n. To be hateful like poison. zahr-mār ; A. tiryāq , adj. Poison-destroying. zahrmär k. , v. n. To swallow as poison. Do chār nivāle zahrmār kar-ke khaṛā ho gayā .

ety: v.n. fr. ظہر 'to be or become outward or exterior,' &c.
def: s.f. The back: the external part, the outer or upper portion, or the outer fold (of a letter, &c.).

n. m. 1. Gall-bladder; bile. Terā kyoṅ zahrah phaṭā? Why has your bile burst? (Why do you burn with envy?) 2. Boldness; spirit. Aisā kiskā zahrā hai jo use roke ? zahrah āb h. , v. n. To be seized by panic; to take fright. zahrah phaṭnā ; H. pitte jalnā , v. n. lit . To crack one's gall bladder. To burn with envy.

ety: of azhar, rt. زهر 'to shine,' &c.
def: s.f. Name of Fātima, daughter of Moḥammad.

ety: fr. zahr, q.v.
def: s.m. Gall-bladder; bile; — boldness, spirit, pluck
more: s. m. 1. Gall-bladder, bile. 2. Boldness. زهره آب هونا zahra āb honā, To be much distressed or terrified.
s. f. Venus (the planet). زهره رو zuhra-rū, adj. (Like) Venus in countenance.
subs: lit. 'The gall to turn to water,' to be much distressed or terrified, to be panic-stricken, to take fright
lit. 'The gall-bladder to burst,' to be consumed with envy (of).

ety: zahr, q.v.+S. aff. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Poisonous, venomous.
n. m. Bezoar; an antidote to poison. zahrīlā, zahrīlī , adj. See bisailā. zahrīlā mādda , n. m. G. G. A poisonous substance.
horoscope; horoscope chart
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زایچه (zāyča).

ety: act. part. of زيد 'to increase,' &c.
def: adj. Exceeding, in excess, beyond, more (than, -se), redundant, superfluous; remaining over and above; additional, in addition; excrescent; adscititious; — useless, worthless

ety: inf. n. of زيد 'to increase'

more: prop. n. A hypothetical name (often used with Amar , as John Nokes with Thomas Stiles, in English).
s. m. 1. Increasing, adding, augmentation. 2. n. prop. A fictitious name (as John Nokes, or Thomas Stiles, in English).

def: adj. زيد increased. 1. Overplus; over; over and above; supplemental; surplus; superfluous ( adhik 1—3). 2. Useless; worthless; waste; vain. parcha-i-zāid , An extra, or extraordinary. zāid-ul-vasf, tārīf bāhar . Beyond description.
more: (part. act. of زيد) adj. Redundant, superfluous, exceeding.

ety: perf. part. of zāʼīdan, fr. zā, rt. of zādan
def: part. Procreated, generated, born; — s.m. One born (of, -kā), offspring, child.
more: n. m. lit . begotten. A son or daughter. Ashrāf kā zāīdah hai to phir na āiyo !

ety: v.n. fr. ضيف; see ẓiyāfat

more: adj. Hospitable, convivial. s. m. A stranger, a guest.

ety: v.n. fr. ضعف; see ẓǒʻf

more: adj. Infirm; feeble. zaīf-ul-eteqād , adj. Credulous. zaīf-ul-aql . Weak-minded. zaīf h. , v. n. To become old, feeble.

(from ضعف) adj. Weak, infirm, impotent, emaciated, feeble, faint, frail, old. ضعيف البنيان ẓaʼīﻌfu-l-bunyān, Frail of foundation. ضعيف الجثه ẓaʼīﻌfu-l-jus̤s̤a, Low in flesh, weak or emaciated in body.

adj. Frail of structure or body
adj. Emaciated in body, low in flesh; insignificant in size or body
adj. In wretched circumstanees, in distress
adj. Of weak or unsound mind, weak-minded

def: (for A. ضعيفة, fem. of ẓaʻīf), adj. fem. = ẓaʻīf, q.v. adj. Weak, feeble, impotent, frail, infirm, emaciated, old; faint, low; unsound; insignificant; poor, wretched, distressed

adj. Frail of structure or body
adj. Emaciated in body, low in flesh; insignificant in size or body

oblique plural of ضَعِیف (za'īf, “poor; old person”)
vocative plural of ضَعِیف (za'īf)

def: adj. & s.m. Biting; — a biter; a lion.
more: part. Biting. s. m. A lion.

def: adj. & s.m. Tasting; — one who tastes, taster.
part. act. (of ذوق) Tasting.
(from ذوق) s. m. The sense of tasting, the palate, relish, taste. ذايقه شناس ẕāʼịka-shinās, Knowing in taste. ذايقه لينا ẕāʼịḳa lenā, To taste.

adj. & s.m. Knowing in taste; — one who is skilled in taste
(-kā), To have a taste (of), to taste, to relish, smack the lips (over).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذَائِقَه (zā'iqa), from Arabic ذَائِقَة (ḏāʔiqa). Related to ذوق (zauq).

oblique plural of ذَائِقَہ (zāiqa, “taste”)
vocative plural of ذَائِقَہ (zāiqa, “taste”)

s.m. The latter, hinder, last, or hindmost part (of anything), lower part, bottom; supplementary part; skirt, or lower extremity (of a garment), train (of a robe); train, retinue; sequel, that which follows, or is annexed
more: s. m. The skirt of a garment, the train of a robe; the last, hinder, or supplementary part of any thing; train, retinue, the sequel, that which follows or is annexed, a list of particulars mentioned in a letter, an appendix.
subs: adv. As below, as follows, as under-mentioned

subs: v.n. To pass away, to cease, fail, &c.

def: (part. act. of زوال) adj. Deficient, failing, perishing, vanishing, waning, declining.
adv. ذيل had a long tail or pendant. As follows; as undermentioned; as proceeding; as below. hasb-i-mufassila zail . As detailed below.
sub, lower part of something
wety: From Arabic ذَيْل (ḏayl).

def: subdistrict

ety: inf. n. of زين 'to adorn'
def: s.m. Adorning, decking; an ornament, a grace, a beauty.
a female given name from Arabic
wety: Borrowed from Arabic زَائِنَة (zāʔina).

def: n. A supposed demon. Kyā tere sir ā chaṛhe chāroṅ ke chāroṅ, alamā! Shāh Daryā, Shaikh Saddo, Zain Khāṅ, Nanne Miyāṅ? Wom. Inshā. What! on your head four demons all alighted, every one, Shekh Saddo, Shāh Daryā, Zain Khān, and Nanneh Miyān beknown!
pilgrim; traveller
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَائِر (zā'ir), borrowed from Arabic زَائِر (zāʔir).

ety: act. part. of زور 'to visit,' &c.
def: part. & s.m. Visiting; — visitor, visitant; pilgrim.
(part. act. of زور) s. m. A visitant; a pilgrim.
s.m. The olive tree; an olive.
wety: From Arabic زَيْتُون (zaytūn) (through Classical Persian زیتون (zaytūn)).

ety: v.n. fr. ضيق; see ẓīq
def: adj. Narrow, strait; contracted; illiberal, niggardly, avaricious; — s.m. A niggard, miser, narrow-hearted man.
(part. act. of ضيع) adj. Lost, destroyed, perished, fruitless, abortive. ضايع کرنا ẓāʼịﻌ ḳarnā, To lose, to destroy, to consume.

ety: orig. zāk or zāg; prob. = S. कृष्णं, cf. zang; A. zāj, fr. the P.

more: s. m. Copperas, vitriol. زاج سفيد zāj-i-sufaid, s. m. Alum.

def: adj. Distressed (in mind), disquieted, vexed, grieved; melancholy; peevish, fretful.

ety: said to be fr. the (prob. post-classical
def: s.f. Defeat, repulse, check; injuring, injury, damage, loss; disgrace, humiliation, insult, affront; — deceiving, baulking, disappointing
more: n. f. زک totter from weakness. 1. Defeat; repulse. 2. Affront; insult; humiliation. 3. Loss; damage; injury. zak uṭhānā, yā pānā , v. n. To suffer humiliation or disgrace. Ab dil ko lagāte haiṅ zarā soch samajh-ke, Do chār jagah pahle jo zak pāë hue haiṅ . Sābir. 2. To suffer loss or damage. zak denā , v. a. 1. To defeat; beat. 2. To put to shame; affront. 3. To cause loss or injury.
s. f. Injuring, deceiving, disappointing. زک اٿهانا zak uṭhānā, To be ashamed. زک دينا zak denā, To put to shame.
subs: v.n. To suffer injury, or loss, or damage; to suffer humiliation or disgrace, to experience shame, be put to shame
(-ko), To beat, defeat, check; to affront; to humiliate, put to shame; to cause injury or loss (to).

more: (from ذکي To blaze, to be acute in mind. Heb. To be pure, clean, splendid) s. m. Acumen, acuteness of genius, penetration, sagacity. ¶ ẕukā, s. m. The sun.

more: n. m. 1. Chin. 2. (Cor, of P. zagand ) Bound; spring. chāh-i-zaqan , Poet, n. m. A dimple in the chin.
(Heb. The beard) s. m. The chin, the beard. چاه ذقن chāh-i-ẕaḳan, s. m. A dimple in the chin.

ety: corr. of P. زغند ẕag̠and, q.v.
def: s.m. Spring, bound.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَکَات (zakāt), ultimately from Arabic زَكَاة (zakāh).

def: alms, charity; Almsgiving; zakat, one of the five pillars of Islam.
wety: Unadapted borrowing from Arabic زَكَوٰة (zakawā), specifically Classical Arabic.

more: (from زکا) s. f. Alms, a portion of a Musalmānʼs property given in charity agreeably to the rules laid down in the ḳurʼān.

def: s.f. = زکاة zakāt, q.v. s.f. Alms, poor-rate, the portion or amount of his property that is given by a Musalman (agreeably to the rules laid down in the Qorʼān) to the poor.
more: n. f. زکو blessed by God. 1. Alms. 2. A portion of a Mussalman's property given in charity agreeably to the Qurān .

ety: for A. ذکاوة, abst. n. fr. ذکو 'to be sharp,' &c.; prob. a post-classical word
def: s.f. Brightness of genius, wit, acuteness, &c. ( = ẕakā, q.v.). s.m. Sharpness or acuteness of mind, quickness of perception or understanding, acumen, sagacity, penetration.
more: n. f. ذکو was sharp. Acuteness; penetration; acumen.
s. f. Brightness of genius, acuteness, acumen, ingenuity.

ety: for A. زکاوة, v.n. fr. زکم 'to be or become pure'
def: s.f. Purity; innocence; probity; ingenuity.
more: (from زکا) s. f. Probity, purity, ingenuity.

def: s.f. pl. (of zakāt), Alms.
s.m. contrac. of زخم zaḵẖm, q.v.
s. plur. (of ذخيره) Victuals, provision, treasures, stores.
s.m. pl. (of ẕaḵẖīra), Treasures, stores, provisions, victuals.

def: s.f. Bigness, thickness, bulkiness.
more: n. f. ضخم large. Thickness; volume; bulk.

def: s. f. See ضخامت ẓaḵẖāmat.

ety: for A. ذخيرة ẕaḵẖīrat, v.n. fr. ذخر 'to hoard, treasure,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

ety: for A. ذخيرة ẕaḵẖīrat, v.n. fr. ذخر 'to hoard, treasure,' &c.
def: s.m. A thing hoarded, treasured, or laid up; treasure, hoard; provisions, victuals, stores, munition
more: n. m. ذخر stored. Provisions (اتَالا 1, 2,); treasure.
s. m. Treasure, provisions, victuals, stores, munition.
subs: v.t. To treasure, store up, lay up.

ety: intens. n. f. زخر 'to rise' (the sea
def: adj. Full and swelling (the sea), overflowing, raging.
more: adj. (superl. from زخر It abounded) Overflowing, raging (the sea).

def: to heal (of wounds)
ex: اِتْنے وَقْت کے بَعْد میرے زَخْم بَھرے ہَیں۔
itne vaqt ke ba'd mere zaxm bhare ha͠i.
my wounds have healed after so much time.

def: to be wounded

ety: prob. jagh˚, fr. Zend rt. jan, redupl.+aff. man; S. जघ, by redupl. fr. rt. हन्+मन्
def: s.m. (f.?), Wound, sore, scar, cut, gash; fracture; injury; damage; loss (syn. ghāʼo; ćoṭ)
more: n. m. 1. A wound; sore; cut; gash ( choṭ , 1.). Bandar kā zakhm . Prov. A monkey's wound. (A wound kept green by constant irritation). 2. Damage; loss. zakhm bharnā , v. n. See bharnā , 12. zakhm par namak chhiṛaknā , v. a. 1. To put salt on one's wound. 2. To afflict the afflicted. zakhm paṛnā , v. n. To become a sore. zakhm paknā , v. n. To suppurate. zakhm-i-talvār , n. m. A sword cut, or wound. zakhm denā , v. a. 1. To give a cut or wound; to stab. 2. To hurt; injure. zakhm-i-kārī , A fatal or mortal wound. zakhm harā h. , v. n. To be renewed (a wound).
s. m. f. A wound, sore, scar, cut. زخم رسا zaḵẖm-rasā, A deep wound. زخم کهانا zaḵẖm khānā, To be wounded. زخم کاري zaḵẖm-i-kārī, A mortal wound.
subs: To sprinkle salt on a wound; to afflict or torture the afflicted
(-ko), To give (one) a wound or cut, &c., to stab; to wound, hurt, injure
or zaḵẖm-kārī, s.m. A mortal wound
A wound to re-open or to be renewed
'A monkey's wound'; a wound kept green by constant irritation
s.m. A wound with the eye, a fascinating look.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَخْم (zaxm).

ety: see zaḵẖm
def: s.m. Plectrum (of a guitar, or lyre, &c.); bow (of a violin, &c.).
more: s. The plectrum of a musical instrument, a small metal instrument for playing on the guitar.

ety: rel. n. fr. zaḵẖm
def: s.m.f. Wounded, hurt; slain, killed by a wound; — a wounded person
more: adj. Wounded; hurt. zakhmī k. , v. a. To wound; hurt. zakhmī h., zakhm khānā , v. n. To be wounded.
n. A wounded man or woman.
subs: v.t. To wound, hurt, to inflict wounds on

ety: v.n. fr. زکو 'to be or become pure'
def: adj. & s.m. Pure, virtuous, good, righteous, pious; continent; one who gives the prescribed portion of his wealth to the poor.
more: (part. from زکا) 1. adj. Pure, pious. 2. Continent. 3. s. m. One who regularly pays the زکاة zakāt, q. v.

ety: rel. n. of ẕakā, q.v.
def: adj. Sharp, acute, intelligent, clever, of penetrating intellect, sagacious (syn. ẕahīn); — fiery, flaming
more: (from ذکا) adj. 1. Acute, ingenious. 2. Fiery, flaming. ذکي المعي ẕakīyu-l-maﻌi, Of a splendid genius.
subs: Of a splendid or brilliant genius.

ety: act. part. n. of ذکر 'to remember,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Remembering; grateful; — rememberer; a grateful person; one who praises God.
more: part. act. (of ذکر) Remembering, grateful, a praiser of God.

ety: v.n. fr. زقم 'to eat quickly,' &c.
def: s.m. An infernal tree (mentioned in the Qorʼān), the fruit of which is supposed to resemble the heads of devils and terrible serpents; — a prickly plant, cactus, Euphorbia antiquorum (?).
more: n. m. A prickly plant; cactus; Euphorbia antiquorum (?). The fruit is supposed to be the heads of devils.
s. A thorny tree so called.

ety: Zend zaurura, fr. zaurva, rt. zar = S. जर्
def: adj. Old, decrepit, grey-headed (man or woman); — name of a famous Persian prince, father of the celebrated Rustam, and son of Sām, son of Narīmān (he was called zāl, or zāl-ě-zar, because he came into the world with white hair, and a red countenance; the former like that of an old man, the latter of golden hue).
more: 1. adj. Old, decrepit, grey-haired (man or woman). 2. n. prop. (or زال زر) The name of the father of Rustam, and son of Sām, son of Narīmān. He was called زال زر ( zāl-i-zar ) because he came into the world with white hair, and a red countenance; the former like that of an old man, the latter of golden hue.

r: ẕāl, dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, th, thou, ze, z., ẕuʼl-ḥijja, abjad
def: called dāl-ě-mǒʻjama, is the thirteenth letter of the Hindūstānī or Urdū alphabet, and ninth of the Arabic (from which it is taken). It is pronounced by the Arabs like th in the English thou; but in Urdū its sound is not to be distinguished from that of ze, which is exactly our own letter z. It is used, by way of abbreviation, for ẕuʼl-ḥijja, the last month of the Arabic lunar year; and in reckoning by abjad, it stands for 700.
more: called dāl-i-mojma , is the ninth letter of the Arabic alphabet. It is pronounced by the Arabs like th in the Eng. word thou ; but in Hindustani its sound is not to be distinguished from that of z . In abjad it stands for 700, and, by way of abbreviation, it is written for ذرالحجه the last month of the Arabic lunar year.

def: hail, hailfall; hail storm
wety: Compound of ژالَہ (źāla, “dew, hail”) + باری (bārī, “stream, flow, spate”)

more: s. m. 1. Erring, blundering. 2. Stumbling, falling. 3. Deficiency, loss.

more: s. m. Errour, fault, vice, ruin, perdition.

ety: v.n. fr. ضلّ 'to err,' &c.
def: s.m. Error, &c. ( = ẓalāl, q.v.). s.m. = ẕull, and ẕillat, q.v. s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).

ety: for A. ذلالة, inf. n. of ذلّ 'to be base,' &c.

s.f. (for A. ضلالة inf. n. of ضلّ), = A ضلال ẓalāl, s.m. (inf. n. of ضلّ 'to err, goastray,' &c.), Erring, going astray — error, fault, vice; ruin, perdition.
more: s. f. Errour, fault, vice, ruin, perdition.

ety: v.n. fr. ظلم; iv اظلم 'to be dark'
def: s.m. Darkness ( = z̤ulmat).
more: s. m. Darkness. ¶ z̤allām (adj. superl.) A great oppressor; very tyrannical.

def: s.m. pl. (of zalzala), Tremblings; earthquakes.

ety: v.n. fr. ذل 'to be base,' &c.
def: adj. Base, mean, vile, wretched, contemptible, despicable, ignominious; brought low, abased, humbled, disgraced, insulted
more: adj. 1. Base; contemptible. 2. Shameless ( be-hayā , 1.). zalīl k .; (Rare) zillat denā , v. a. 1. To insult; hoot at; shame. 2. To disgrace; degrade. 3. To dishonor; defile. zalīl h , v. n. To be insulted, disgraced.
(from ذلّ) adj. Abject, submissive, mean, contemptible, wretched, base. ذليل کرنا ẕalīl karnā, To abase, to debase.
subs: v.t. To abase, debase, disgrace, render abject or vile, to humble, bring low, to insult
v.n. To be brought low, be abased, be disgraced, be insulted.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ذلیل (zalīl), from Arabic ذَلِيل (ḏalīl). Compare Punjabi ذليل / ਜ਼ਲੀਲ (zalīl).

ety: v.n. fr. ظلّ 'to shade'
def: adj. Having shade, shady, affording shelter.

ety: act. part. of ظلم 'to act wrongfully or tyrannically'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Acting wrongfully, tyrannical, unjust, cruel; — a tyrant, an oppressor, a wrong-doer, a cruel person; (met.) an unrelenting sweetheart, a cruel mistress
more: n. m. ظلم did wrong. 1. A tyrant ( sitamgār ). 1. Zālim kī umar kotāh . Prov. The tyrant's term of life is short. 2. Jo ki zālim hai woh hargiz phūltā phaltā nahīṅ, Sabz hote khet dekhā hai kahīṅ shamshīr kā? Nāsikh. No tyrant yet a golden harvest e'er could own, Who ever saw the waving corn by sword yet grown? 3. Darvāzah maë-kade kā na kar band, mohtasib; Zālim, Khudā se ḍar! ki dar-i-taubā bāz hai . Zauq. The door of taverns free thou public censor do not bar! Tyrant! fear God who leaves the door of penitence ajar. 4. Zālim kā zor sir par . Prov. The tyrant smites on the head. 2. ( Poet .) A cruel mistress. Nainā tere zālim zor, manhiyār kī! Song. Your eyes possess a cruel force, oh bangle-seller fair! zāl'im ; Illit. zālam , adj. 1. See (انيائي 3). 2. Cruel; barbarous; wanton. Zālam jaṭṭī ne toṛe haiṅ ber, dhele ke pāo ser, jhāṛī būṭī ke ber! Delhī Street cry. Plums plucked by a crue…
(part. act. of ظلم) 1. s. m. A tyrant, an oppressor. 2. adj. Tyrannical, unjust. 3. ( met. ) Applied to a beloved person or mistress. ظالم گداز z̤ālim-gudāz, A destroyer of the tyrannical.
subs: s.m. A destroyer of tyrants or oppressors (applied to a ruler who maintains justice and good order).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic ظَالِم (ẓālim).

def: feminine of ظَالِم (zālim)

ety: z̤ālim, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Wrong-doing, tyranny, oppression ( = z̤ulm).

ety: v.n. fr. ذلق 'to be sharp or eloquent'
def: s.m. A sharp and ready or eloquent tongue; readiness of speech, volubility
more: (Heb. ) s. m. A prompt and ready or eloquent tongue. حرف الذلق ḥarfu-ẕ-ẕalḳ, Lingual letter; as ل رن.

ety: inf. n. of ضلّ 'to err,' &c.
def: s.m. Erring, going astray, &c. ( = ẓalāl, q.v.). s.m. = ẕull, and ẕillat, q.v. s.m. Baseness, meanness, &c. (see ẕillat, and next).
more: s. m. An errour, a vice.

ety: act. part. of ضلّ 'to err, go astray,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Erring, straying, going astray; becoming lost; — forgetting; — a prevaricator, &c.
more: part. act. (of ضلّ) 1. Straying. 2. s. m. A prevaricator.

ety: for A. زلة, v.n. fr. زلّ 'to slip,' &c.
def: s.m. Food carried home by the guests from an entertainment.
more: s. m. Victuals carried home by the guests from an entertainment.

ety: intens. n. fr. ضلّ; see ẓall
def: adj. Going greatly astray, erring extremely.

ety: v.n. intens. fr. ظلم 'to act wrongfully,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Very unjust or tyrannical; — a great tyrant or oppressor.

ety: = S. जालुका
def: s.f. A leech.

ety: v.n. fr. ذلّ 'to be easy,' &c.
def: adj. Gentle, tractable, docile, submissive, obedient.

ety: intens. adj. fr. ظلم; see z̤ulm
def: adj. & s.m. Acting wrongfully, most oppressive, tyrannical, cruel; — one who acts wrongfully, &c.; a grievous wrong-doer.
more: adj. (superl. of ظلم) Cruel, most oppressive, tyrannical.

ety: for A. زلزلة, inf. n. of زلزل 'to put in a state of motion'
def: s.m. Violent motion, commotion, convulsion; earthquake.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَلْزَلَه (zalzala), from Arabic زَلْزَلَة (zalzala). First attested in c. 1739 as Middle Hindi زلزلہ (zlzlh /⁠zalzala⁠/).

def: direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
vocative plural of زَلْزَلَہ (zalzala, “earthquake”)
oblique plural of زَلْزَلَہ (zalzala, “earthquake”)
s.m. pl. (of ẕamīma), Crimes, misdemeanours; reprehensible or base qualities.
s. plur. of ضمير ẓamīr, q. v.
s.f. pl. (of ẓamīr, q.v.), Personal pronouns, &c.
s.m. Time ( = zamān, q.v.). s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.

ety: inf. n. of ضمن 'to be or become responsible,' &c.
def: s.m. Suretiship, guaranteeship, security (see next).
Zaman Park (a neighbourhood of Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan)
wety: Named after Post Master General of Punjab Khan Bahadur Mohammad Zaman Khan, from زَمان (zamān) + پارْک (pārk).

ety: v.n. fr. iv of زمن
def: s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
more: (Heb. from زمن Heb. To define, to determine, to appoint) s. m. 1. Time, an age. 2. The world. 3. Fortune. 4. The heavens. 5. Tense. (Plur. ازمنه). زمانه ساز zamāna-sāz, Turncoat, time-server, temporizer. زمانه سازي zamāna-sāz-ī, Turning with the tide, timeserving, yielding to fortune. زمانه سازي کرنا zamāna-sāz-ī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize. زمانه موافق zamāna-muwāfiḳ, Fortunate.
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.

ety: for A. زمانة, from zamān
def: s.m. Time, period, duration; season; a long time; an age; (in Gram.) tense; — the world; the heavens; fortune, destiny
more: n. m. Z. damān ; A. زمن stayed a long time. 1. Time; period; duration. 2. An age (H. jug ). Āj kal achchhe kā zamāna nahīṅ hai . 3. The world. Zamāne meṅ to tum sarīkā hogā nahīṅ! No one like you in the world! 4. ( Gram .) Tense or time (H. kāl ). zamāna dekhnā , v. a. To see the world; to get experience. zamāna-sāz , n. m. A time-server (خوشامدي). zamāna-sāzī , n. f. Time-serving; sycophancy. zamāne ke muāfiq . According to the fashion of the day. zamāna muāfiq h . To be fortunate.

(Heb. from زمن Heb. To define, to determine, to appoint) s. m. 1. Time, an age. 2. The world. 3. Fortune. 4. The heavens. 5. Tense. (Plur. ازمنه). زمانه ساز zamāna-sāz, Turncoat, time-server, temporizer. زمانه سازي zamāna-sāz-ī, Turning with the tide, timeserving, yielding to fortune. زمانه سازي کرنا zamāna-sāz-ī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize. زمانه موافق zamāna-muwāfiḳ, Fortunate.
subs: To see the world, to gain experience
s.m. A time-server, temporizer, turn-coat, sycophant
s.f. Time-serving, turning with the tide, yielding to fortune; sycophancy; hypocrisy; necessity; — zamāna-sāzī karnā, To turn with the tide, to temporize, &c.
According to the times, according to the fashion of the day
adj. Well-timed; favoured by the times or by fortune, fortunate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَمَانَه (zamāna). Equivalent to زَمان (zamān) + ـہ (-h). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi زمانہ (zmanh).

ety: for A. ضمانة, fr. ẓamān
def: s.f. Security, &c. ( = ẓāminī, q.v.)
more: n. f. Security (ضامني). zamānat-i-hifz-i-aman, yā adam-fasād , Security to keep the peace. zamānat dākhil k., yā denā . To furnish security; give bail. zamānat-i-kāfī . Sufficient security. zamānat ke qābil . Bailable. zamānat le-kar chhoṛnā, zamānat pe rihā k. , v. a. To discharge on bail; to hold to bail. zamānat-nāmah , n. m. Bail bond; security bond; a deed of suretyship. zamānat-i-nek-chalanī , n. f. Security for good behaviour. gair qābil-ul-zamānat . Not bailable.
subs: v.t. To discharge on bail, to hold to bail
or ẓamānat denā, To furnish security, give bail
s.m. Bail-bond, security-bond, deed of suretiship.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضمانت (zamānat), from Arabic ضَمَانَة (ḍamāna) / ضَمان (ḍamān).

ety: for A. ضمانةً, acc. of ẓamānat
def: adv. By way of security.
more: n. m. One who gives bail or security.

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓamānat
def: s.m. One who gives bail or security (syn. ẓāmin, and ẓamānat dene-wālā).
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
oblique plural of زَمانَہ (zamāna, “era; world; reign”)
vocative plural of زَمانَہ (zamāna, “era; world; reign”)

ety: v.n. fr. ذنب 'to follow the tail, or the track of'
def: s.m. Sin, crime, fault, misdemeanour, misdeed.
more: s. m. A crime, sin, fault. (Plur. ذنوب ẕunūb ). ¶ ẕanab (Heb. ) s. m. 1. A tail. 2. The descending node.

def: s.m. The white jasmine, Jasminum sambac; the iris, Iris germanica, or I. sambac; the lily.
more: s. Name of a flower, the white jasmine, a lily, or an iris.

ety: A. zabīl, zambīl, zimbīl
def: s.f. A basket (made of palm-leaves); a dried and hollow gourd (for keeping things in); a purse, bag, wallet.
more: n. f. A bag; wallet. Amar aiyār kī zambīl . The horn of plenty.
s. f. 1. A basket. 2. A purse, a wallet. (Adopted in Arab. plur. زنابيل).

ety: prob. corr. of زنبور, q.v.
def: s.m. (local), A vice, nippers, forceps.
more: s. m. A vice, nippers, forceps.

ety: for A. zumbur
def: s.m. A hornet; bee, wasp; a vice, &c. (see زمبور zambūr); — a camel-swivel ( = zambūrak).
more: n. m. 1. A wasp. 2. Bell-mouthed guns carried on camels. 3. Pincers; dental forceps.
(Chald. Heb. from To lead, to speak; so named, perhaps, from following its leader; or, from its humming noise) s. m. f. 1. A hornet. 2. A bee. (Plur. زنابير). See, also, H. زمبور zambūr.

def: s.m. A hornet; a large bee; — a camel-swivel; — a kind of guitar made of two gourds (syn. kingrī); the point of this instrument.
more: s. m. 1. A hornet, large bee. 2. The point of a musical stringed instrument called in Hindī کنگري. 3. A small gun. زنبورچي zambūr-chī, A fusileer.

ety: zambūr+dim. aff. ak = S. अक
def: s.f. A small gun or cannon, a camel-swivel ( = zambūr, and zambūra).
more: s. f. A small gun or swivel, usually carried on a camel.

def: s.m. A fusileer.
s.m. Intense cold; cold.
more: (from زمـہر The eye being red with anger) s. m. Cold, intense cold.

ety: from the preceding
def: adj. Intensely cold.

ety: Pārsī zamī; Pehl. damīk; Zend zem; S. जम्
def: s.f. The earth, &c. ( = zamīn), q.v.
subs: s.m. = zamīn-dār; and zamī-dārī, s.f. = zamīn-dārī; qq.v.s.v. zamīn.

def: corrupt. of زميندار zamīn-dār, q. v. Landlord, &c.

ety: for A. ضميمة, n. of un. fr. ضميم, v.n. fr. ضمّ 'to collect, to contract'
def: s.m. An adjunct, addition, augmentation; a thing incorporated (in another); an appendix (syn. tatimma), a supplement.
more: n. m. 1. An appendix ( tatimmā ). 2. An extra, a supplement to a newspaper.
s. m. Addition, thing incorporated, incorporation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَمِیمَه (zamīma).

ety: for A. ذميمة ẕamīmat, fr. ẕamīm, 'blamed'; rt. ذمّ 'to blame'
def: s.m. An act, or a quality, to be blamed or reprehended; a reprehensible act, bad quality, vice, misdeed, crime, misdemeanour.

ety: Zend zemaênya, fr. zem; see zamī
def: s.f. The earth; soil, land, ground; floor; foundation, ground-work; the ground of a picture; region, country
more: n. f. Z. zamīk , Vedic S. jam . 1. See بهوم 1. Unkā kyā ṭhikānā hai, kabhī zamīn meṅ, kabhī āsmān meṅ . There's no holding him, his feet now on the earth, now in the skies. 2. Zamīn meṅ lāt māre to pānī nikal paṛe ! Wom. He is so strong that a kick from his foot would. make water issue from the earth! 2. The ground of a picture. 3. Metre. Zamīn-i-sher. zamīn āsmān kā farq . A great difference. zamīn āsmān ke gullābe milānā , v. a. lit . to make the sky and earth meet. To exaggerate; tell great lies. zamīṅ-bos h. , v. n. To kiss the ground; prostrate oneself. zamīn par se kuchh paṛā pānā . See āsmān se girnā. zamīn pakaṛnā , v. n. To stick to a place.

s. f. The earth, ground, soil, land, floor, a region, country, the ground of a picture. زمين کاهونا zamīn kā honā (dakh. i. q. لاچارهونا) To be helpless or destitute (?). زمين بوس zamīn-bos honā, To make a profound bow. زمين پرسي کچهہ پڙاپانا zamīn par se kuchh paṛā pānā, To be overjoyed at finding unexpectedly the object of oneʼs wishes. زمين پکڙني zamīn pakaṛnī, To persevere obstinately in any design, to insist. زمين گير zamīn-gīr, adj. Oppressed or broken down by age or time, paralytick. زمين مين گڙجانا zamīn meṅ gaṛ jānā, To be greatly ashamed.
subs: 'As wide asunder as heaven and earth'; a very great difference
v.n. To kiss the ground, to prostrate oneself; to make a profound bow, to make obeisance
To obtain a thing without exertion or labour; to find or obtain a thing unexpectedly; to be overjoyed at finding unexpectedly the object of one's wishes
To be rooted to a spot; to stick to a place; to persevere obstinately in a design, to stick (to a thing), to insist
adj. Level with the ground; — subterranean
s.m. A kind of sweet potato
v.n. 'To become a patch or piece of the earth,' to mingle with the dust, to die
s.f. 'A neck of land,' an isthmus (syn. ḵẖāk-nāʼe; ḍamru-madh)

adj. & s.m. Falling to the ground; taking to the ground; hanging down to the ground, bent low; bowed; oppressed or broken down by age or time; paralytic; — one who is bowed or bent double, a paralytic
'To fit, or to be put, into the ground,' to be buried
lit. 'To be buried in the earth'; to sink into the earth with shame, to be greatly ashamed
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زَمِین (zamīn). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi زمین (zmyn /⁠zamīn⁠/). Compare Punjabi زمین (zamīn), Sindhi زَمِينُ (zamīnu).

ety: act. part. of ضمن 'to be or become responsible for,' &c.
def: s.m. One who is responsible or accountable (for), a surety, guarantee; security, sponsor, bail, bondsman; — a small piece of wood connecting the two tubes of a ḥuqqa
more: n. m. ضمن was responsible. 1. A surety. Girah kā dije, zāmin na hūje . Prov. Your money you may hand, but surety never stand! 2. A small piece of wood connecting the two tubes of a huqqa. 3. (Ped. for H. jāman , q. v.) Rennet. zāmin dar zāmin . A collateral security. zāmin kī barīat . Discharge of a surety. zāmin h. , n. m. To be surety for another; to give or stand bail; to guarantee; engage for another. zāmin denā , v. a. To give a surety. fel-zāmin . A surety or security for good conduct or for a person's refraining from any proscribed offence or misdemeanor. māl-zāmin . A security for the discharge of a debt; recognizances; bail.
(part. act. of ضمن) s. m. A surety, a sponsor, security, bail, bondsman.
subs: s.m. One having a bail or surety, person bailed
(-kā), To be surety (for), to give or stand bail (for); to guarantee; to engage (for)
s.m. A surety or security for good conduct, or for (a person's) refraining from any prescribed offence or misdemeanour
s.m. A security for the discharge of a debt; recognizances; bail.

def: Entirely different, opposite (night and day); Used other than figuratively or idiomatically: see زَمِین (zamīn), آسمان (āsmān).

ety: zamīn+dār, qq.v.
def: s.m. Land-holder, land-owner, landlord, landed proprietor; farmer.
more: n. m. A landholder; landlord; landed proprietor; landowner; lord of a manor. Zimīndār ko kisān, bachche ko masān ! Prov. The tenant to a landlord, merasmus to a child! zamīṅ-dārah , n. 1. The allowance to a zamīn-dār who is set aside. 2. Country ( dihāt ). zamīṅ-dār aur jotā . Landlord and tenant. zamīṅ-dār'nī, zamīn-dāran , n. f. The wife of a zamīn-dār .
(زمين Ground, and part. act. of داشتن To have, to hold) A landlord, a landholder, a farmer.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زمیندار (zamīn-dār).

ety: or zamīndār-āna
def: s.m. Allowance made to a zamīndār who is set aside; — country (syn. dihāt).

ety: zamīndār+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a zamīndār.
s.f. Landed estate, landed property, estate, tenure in severalty, fee-farm, freehold, fief; land or estate held by a zamīndār; the office or tenure of a zamīndār
more: n. f. 1. Real or landed estate; landed property; estate; tenure in severalty; fee farm; freehold; manor; fief. 2. The office or tenure of a zamīn-dār ; the land or estates held by him. zamīṅ-dārī paṭṭā , n. m. A document granted by Government in recognition of the possessions of a zamīn-dār. zamīṅ-dārī jama , n. f. The amount of revenue which the holder of a zamīn-dārī has engaged to pay to the Government. zamīn-doz , adj. 1. Level with the ground. 2. Subterranean ( bhūm tale ). zamī dekhnā , v. a. Wom. To vomit ( qai k .). zamīn-i-gair-mazrūā , n. f. Uncaltivated land. zamīn kā paivand h. , Wom. v. n. To mingle with the dust; to die. zamīn kā karāyā mahsūl . Revenue from land; land rent. zamīn kā gaz , A great traveller. zamīn kā mālik , n. m. A landowner. zamīn khodnā , v. a. To dig; excavate. zamīn kī bhej . Ground rent. zamīn kī gardan ; P. khāknāë ; S. डमरु मध्य ( Geog .) Isthmus. z…
s. f. A landed estate, a fief.
subs: s.m. A document granted by Government in recognition of the estate of a zamīndār
s.f. The amount of revenue which the holder of a zamīndārī has engaged to pay to the Government.

ety: zamīndār+S. aff. इनी
def: s.f. The wife of a zamīndār.
more: s. f. The wife of a landholder or farmer.

def: direct plural of زَمِین (zamīn)
adj. Of or relating to the earth, earthly, earthy, terrestrial.
adj. Of or relating to the earth; earthly; terrestrial.

ety: ẓāmin, q.v. + ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Guarantee, security, surety, bail; pledge
more: n. f. Security; bail; pledge; guarantee. zāmnī par chhoṛnā, zāmnī manzūr k. , v. a. To admit to, or accept bail. zar-i-zāminī , n. m. Money paid as a security. zanjīr-zāminī , n. f. Chain security. A number of persons binding themselves severally or jointly for each other; the joint responsibility of the cultivators of a village or district for the whole revenue or for money borrowed of a banker. fel-zāminī , n. f. Security to keep the peace or for good conduct. māl-zāminī , n. f. Surety for the payment of any pecuniary obligation.
s. f. Surety, bail. ضامني قبول کرنا ẓāmin-ī ḳabūl karnā, To admit as bail.
subs: (-ko), To release on bail, to admit to bail
or ẓāminī manz̤ūr karnā (-kī), To accept the security or bail (of), to admit as bail

s.f. 'Chain-security,' a number of persons binding themselves severally or jointly for one another; joint responsibility (as of the cultivators of a village or district for the whole revenue thereof, or for money borrowed, &c.)
s.f. Security for good conduct, or to keep the peace, &c.
s.f. Surety for the payment of any pecuniary obligation.

def: oblique plural of زَمِین (zamīn)
vocative plural of زَمِین (zamīn)

ety: v.n. fr. اضمر 'to suppress,' &c., iv of ضمر 'to be lean'
def: s.f. A personal pronoun; — s.m. and f. Heart, mind; the recesses of the mind, the secret thoughts; thought, reflection, sense, conscience, conception, idea, comprehension.
more: n. f. 1. ( Ped .) The mind; heart. 2. ( Gram .) A pronoun.
s. f. 1. The mind, heart, thought, reflection, sense, conscience, conception, idea, comprehension. 2. A personal pronoun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضَمِیر (zamīr), from Arabic ضَمِير (ḍamīr). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi ضمیر (zamīr).

ety: rel. n. fr. ẓamīr
def: adj. Concealed; of or relating to the mind or thought; pronominal.
more: adj. Of the mind or thought, pronominal.

def: adj. Of or belonging to winter; wintry.

ety: inf. n. of ذمّ 'to blame'
def: s.f. Blame, censure, reproach, accusation, disparagement, detraction ( = maẕammat, which is the more common form).
more: s. f. Blame, reproach, accusation, detraction.

ety: inf. n. of ضمّ 'to draw together, to contract,' &c.
def: s.m. Contraction; conjunction; incorporation; — the vowel-sound u (pronounced like the English u in push; — cf. ẓamma).
more: n. m. The vowel point (ُ) pronounced as the Italian short u or as the English u in full ( pesh , 2).
(Chald. To contract, to draw together) s. m. 1. Contraction, conjunction, incorporation. 2. The vowel point (ُ) pronounced as the Italian short u or as the English u in full.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ضم (zamm), from Arabic ضَمّ (ḍamm).

ety: for A. ضمة, inf. n. of un. fr. ضمّ; see ẓamm
def: s.m. The sign () of the vowel-sound u (termed ẓamm; cf. pesh).

ety: v.n. fr. رزمزم 'to make a confused sound'
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated well at Mecca, called Hagar's well.
more: s. m. The name of a well at Mecca, called also Hagarʼs well.

ety: for A. زمزمة, inf. n. of زمزم; see zamzam
def: s.m. Singing, chanting, intoning; chant; modulation; hum, a low murmuring sound
more: n. m. زم a distant sound. Singing; chanting; modulation of the voice. zam zam is the refrain of some English and Italian songs. zamzama-pardāz , n. m. A singer; songster.
(quadril. inf. i. of زمزم) s. m. Singing, modulation, a concert. زمزمه پرداز zamzama-pardāz, Singer, songster.

def: s.f. A goblet or vessel filled with water from the well Zamzam, q.v.
more: n. f. A goblet filled with water from the zamzam well at Mecca.

ety: Pehl. zan; Zend ghena; S. ग्ना, for ज्ञा
def: s.f. Woman; wife
more: ( In comp .) Striking; as rah-zan a highwayman.
n. f. A woman; a wife (استري). zan-murīd , adj. Henpecked ( jorū kā mazūr ). zan-i-mankūhah . A married woman (Opp. of mad/?/khālā , a concubine). be-zan o farzand . Without wife and children.

1. s. f. A woman, a wife. زن کرنا يا لينا zan-karnā or -lenā, To marry. زن مريد zan-murīd, adj. Hen-pecked. 2. part. act. (from زدن To beat, to strike) in compos. Beating, striking, beater, striker; as, رهزن rah-zan, A highwayman.
subs: or zan lenā, To take a wife, to marry
s.f. A married woman, a lawful wife (as opp. to zan-ě-madḵẖūla, 'concubine')
wety: PIE word *gʷḗn Borrowed from Classical Persian زن (zan, “woman; wife”). Cognate with English queen.

ety: rt. of zadan; Zend jan; S. हन्
def: part. & s.m. Beating, striking, smiting; — beater, striker (used as last member of compounds, e.g. rah-zan, 'high wayman').
wety: PIE word *gʷḗn Borrowed from Classical Persian زن (zan, “woman; wife”). Cognate with English queen.

ety: v.n. fr. ذنب; see ẕamb
def: s.m. Tail; end, extremity; (in Astron.) the tail of the Dragon, the descending node.

ety: prob. akin to S. गण्ड
def: s.m. The chin; the pit in the chin.
more: s. m. The chin, the pit in the chin.

def: s. (dakh.) 1. i. q. سانپ sāmp, A snake. 2. i. q. بند کفن band-i-kafan, The fastening or band of a shroud (?).

ety: prob. akin to S. गण्ड
def: s.m. The chin; the pit in the chin.
more: n. The chin. chāh-i-zanakh-dāṅ , Poet. A dimple on the chin.
s. m. The chin, the pit in the chin.

def: n. m. See dugānah 3, under do.

ety: fr. zadan; see zan
def: part. Beating, striking; striking up (used, like zan, in comp.).

ety: of zan
def: s.m. Men who affect the speech and ways of women.
more: part. pres. (from زدن) Striking, beating, striking up.

ety: zan+āna = Zend ana, or āna = S. अन; or zanān+a = Zend a = S. a
def: adj. & s.m. Female, feminine; womanly; effeminate; — an effeminate person, a woman; a eunuch (an individual of a class whose occupation is singing and dancing, &c.); — women's apartments, seraglio, harem; — women, wives.
more: adj. 1. Female; feminine. 2. Womanish; effeminate. 3. Weak; imbecile; impotent. Mue zanāne merī qadr, ūhī! kyā jāneṅ? Mere to āne kī haiṅ mard ārzū karte . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Pish! what knows he of my rare worth? the wretched impotent! A man would burn to feel my passion's liquid fire bespent. zanāna madarsah . Ped. A girl's school. zanāna-makān, zanān-khānah . The female apartments. zanān'a , n. m. 1. A woman; a female. Hai zanāna, bhes mardāna? Prov. Her sex female, dressed as a male! 2. A family; a wife. Savārī kī savārī, zanānā sāth! (Fac.) Said to one who rides a mare. An animal to ride, a wife beside. 3. The female apartments. 4. A eunuch who is also a catamite ( hījṛā ). zanāne , n. m. A class of eunuchs whose occupation is singing and dancing and sodomy.
1. adj. Female, feminine, effeminate, womanly. 2. s. m. Female apartments, a seraglio.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian زنانه. By surface analysis, انہ + زن

ety: zanān, pl. of zan, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Of or belonging to women, feminine; of or relating to a seraglio.
more: adj. Belonging to women; feminine. zanānī bolī ; Poet. r/?/khtī , n. f. Women's language.
adj. Of the women, of the seraglio.

def: n. m. Men who affect the speech and style of women.
huge, giant; old, worn-out; patched, raggy
